Center: US National Nuclear Data Center, Brookhaven, USA
ref: Jour: Physical Review, Part C, Nuclear Physics, Vol.109, p.015802 (2024)
title: Elastic scattering of 3He + 4He with the SONIK scattering chamber

x4freetext: Elastic scattering of 3He + 4He with the SONIK

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Physical Review, Part C, Nuclear Physics, Vol.109, p.015802 (2024)

x4codes.hlp: Ohio University, Athens, OH, United States of America
Tri University Meson Facility, Vancouver, B.C., Canada
Colorado School of Mines, Golden, CO, United States of America
Simon Frazer University, Burnaby, B.C., Canada
Universidad Autonoma de Madrid, Madrid, Spain
Univ. of British Columbia, Vancouver, B.C., Canada
Univ. of Alberta, Edmonton, Alberta, Canada
Tri University Meson Facility, Vancouver, B.C., Canada

x4freetext: An array of 30 collimated silicon charged
particle detectors. The charged particle detectors
are mounted in an assembly referred to as the
detector telescope. The detector telescopes are
arranged such that they look into three different
points, named as interaction regions, in the gas
target along the beam direction (z direction). Since
each interaction region has a different z coordinate,
the bombarding energy and therefore the scattering
energy varies slightly by interaction region. Ideally,
one could measure differential cross sections at
three different energies with a single bombarding
Scattering of Nuclei in Inverse Kinematics (SONIK)
scattering chamber: a windowless, extended gas target.
at 9 energies. Since the projectile and the target
mass are close, authors observed both the recoils and
ejectiles from the elastic scattering in the
detectors. The 3He and 4He peaks are well resolved at
high incident beam energies. The individual peak
yields from the raw spectrum were extracted after
background subtraction. The yields for both the 3He
and 4He peaks were calculated whenever possible.

x4code.headerHelp: Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: effects of statistical and certain systematic
gamma in MeV**0.5 are listed in the article. These
values were converted in to gamma**2 in MeV. From a
propagation formula: the error of the squared
parameter is approximately twice the original
parameter`s value multiplied by its original error.
